perturbation.original_text: In the event this Agreement is terminated for any reason, MICOA agrees to purchase from Agency, and Agency agrees to sell to MICOA Agency's ownership interest in the expirations for the MICOA insurance issued pursuant to this Agreement. The purchase price shall be two times Agency's commissions on business produced directly by Agency during the last 12 full months preceding the termination date. The purchase shall be completed within 60 calendar days after the termination date. In return for this payment, for a two-year period following the termination date, Agency will not directly or indirectly sell any professional liability insurance to any individuals or entities who were MICOA insureds in Nevada at the time of termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event this Agreement is terminated for any reason, MICOA agrees to purchase from Agency, and Agency agrees to sell to MICOA Agency's ownership interest in the expirations for the MICOA insurance issued pursuant to this Agreement. The purchase price shall be one times Agency's commissions on business produced directly by Agency during the last 12 full months preceding the termination date. The purchase shall be completed within 90 calendar days after the termination date. In return for this payment, for a two-year period following the termination date, Agency will not directly or indirectly sell any professional liability insurance to any individuals or entities who were MICOA insureds in Nevada at the time of termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering both the purchase price multiplier for expirations (from two times to one times) and the completion timeframe (from 60 to 90 days). This contradicts the overarching principle established in Section A, outlining the Agency's authority and the commissions detailed in Exhibit A. Since the calculation of the expiration purchase price relies on the commission structure defined in Exhibit A, changing the multiplier directly impacts the financial expectations tied to the commission, creating a legal ambiguity regarding the accurate financial settlement upon termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. AUTHORITY OF AGENCY Subject to requirements imposed by law, the underwriting rules, procedures and regulations of MICOA and this agreement, the Agency is authorized to: 1. Solicit within the State of Nevada, receive and transmit immediately and directly to MICOA, proposals for health care liability insurance contracts for which a commission is specified in the schedule of commissions provided by Exhibit A, attached and as amended or supplemented by such attachments from time to time.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the financial terms (purchase price multiplier and completion timeframe) related to the agency's commission, which directly impacts the authority of the agency and the commission structure as specified in Exhibit A (Contradicted Text). By altering the multiplier and completion timeframe, the change creates a contradiction because the calculation of the expiration purchase price relies on the commission structure defined in Exhibit A. This violates the pre-agreed commission described in Exhibit A, leading to a strong in-text contradiction and potentially altering financial obligations.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall terminate, subject to any automatic renewal or extension for one year as required by law, upon either party giving at least one hundred twenty (120) days advance written notice to the other, if not otherwise contrary to applicable law or this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall terminate, subject to any automatic renewal or extension for one year as required by law, upon either party giving at least ninety (90) days advance written notice to the other, if not otherwise contrary to applicable law or this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the advance written notice period required for termination from 120 days to 90 days. This directly contradicts Section A, specifically A.8, which outlines conditions related to subagencies and their compliance requirements. The original 120-day notice period provided a longer timeframe for orderly transitions, including potentially terminating subagency agreements. The reduced 90-day period creates uncertainty whether sufficient time exists to terminate a subagency without causing contractual breaches or disrupting service to insureds, conflicting with the intention of A.8 to ensure a smooth operation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Agency may solicit subagencies for appointment, subject to MICOA's prior written approval of each subagency following disclosure to and review by MICOA of information requested by MICOA for each proposed subagency. All such appointments by Agent shall stipulate that MICOA may terminate the subagency at any time without cause upon at least 90 days notice and that the subagency shall comply with all MICOA requirements and duties owed MICOA by Agency concerning solicitation, communications, and service to insureds. Subagencies shall also be required to submit all proposals immediately and directly to MICOA.
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the termination notice period from 120 days to 90 days. The contradicted text (Section 8) specifies that MICOA may terminate subagency agreements with at least 90 days' notice. This creates a contradiction because the agency now has less time to terminate the main agreement which might have the implication of not being able to terminate subagencies within that time, potentially conflicting with the MICOA's right to terminate subagencies with at least 90 days notice. The agency agreement requires potentially longer period of 120 days (now 90) and subagency agreement requires at least 90 days to allow termination. Changing the 120 days to 90 days in the agency agreement, creates a direct conflict regarding timing for the agency agreement.
